questions.question: Which setting restricts user logins to specific network locations?

choices.label: A
choices.explanation: IP ranges restrict where users can log in from.

choices.label: B
choices.explanation: Profiles handle object/field access, not network restriction directly.

choices.label: C
choices.explanation: Sharing rules grant record access.

choices.label: D
choices.explanation: PSGs aggregate permissions, not network rules.

questions.explanation: Login IP Ranges (on profiles or org-wide) restrict permitted login networks.

questions.question: If OWD for an object is Private, which feature most easily opens read access to an entire role hierarchy?

choices.label: A
choices.explanation: Manual is record-by-record.

choices.label: B
choices.explanation: Role hierarchy automatically grants upward access (when 'Grant Access Using Hierarchies' is on).

choices.label: C
choices.explanation: Used with sharing rules; not automatic.

choices.label: D
choices.explanation: Requires custom code.

questions.explanation: Role hierarchy is the simplest mechanism for upward access through management chains.

questions.question: Which setting hides specific fields from a user's UI and API access?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Field-Level Security on the Profile or Permission Set
choices.explanation: FLS controls field visibility.

choices.label: B
choices.explanation: Layouts can hide on the page but don't enforce API security.

choices.label: C
choices.explanation: Validation rules block save; not visibility.

choices.label: D
choices.explanation: Sharing rules govern records, not fields.

questions.explanation: FLS on profiles or permission sets controls both UI and API field access.

questions.question: Which Salesforce capability enforces a second factor for user logins via TOTP, push, or SMS?

choices.label: A
choices.explanation: That's challenge-on-unrecognized-device, not enforced MFA.

choices.label: B
choices.explanation: MFA enforces an additional verification factor.

choices.label: C
choices.explanation: Session settings handle timeouts, not MFA enforcement directly.

choices.label: D
choices.explanation: Password policy is single-factor.

questions.explanation: MFA, required by Salesforce for all production logins, adds a second factor.

questions.question: What is the modern Salesforce recommendation for assigning permissions to users?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Use only Profiles and avoid Permission Sets
choices.explanation: Salesforce is moving permissions OFF profiles.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Use minimal Profiles plus Permission Sets / Permission Set Groups
choices.explanation: Recommended pattern: lean profile + assignable permission sets.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Grant every user the System Administrator profile
choices.explanation: Massive security risk.

choices.label: D
choices.explanation: Sharing rules govern records, not permissions.

questions.explanation: Lean profiles + permission sets/groups is the recommended modern pattern.
